main_event: Firey ball traveling south to north on curved track, very large, observed for about four seconds at approximately 25 degrees altitude from position 35.5N 8.01.5W (between Portugal and Morocco) on 22 April 1959.
sighted_object: firey ball; very large; curved track; south to north; altitude about 25 degrees; bearing from 250 to 290 degrees
witness_description: Crew of SS Ocean Star/King of Trition SS, 25 Broadway NYC; ships course 270 degrees, speed 11.12 knots
interesting_points: Report logged 22 April 1959 but only later reported; received by WCC at 1159 GMT, relayed 27 April
Ship initially considered whether object might be Discoverer II satellite (1959A); routing slip from Lt Hamilton confirms Discoverer II was still in orbit after 22 April, ruling it out
Routing slip to Maj. Friend (4E4): 'The subject of this report is not a satellite'
Position: LAT 35.5N LONG 8.01.5W; ships course 270 degrees, speed 11.12 knots
Record card comments: 'description is typical of a meteor'
